name: Thought Machine Vault Core Banking API
what_it_does: Thought Machine Vault Core Banking REST API for cloud-native core banking infrastructure platform. Enables AI agents to manage Smart Contract-based financial product configuration and deployment, handle account lifecycle management including creation, modification, and closure, access real-time balance and transaction data for all product types, retrieve posting instruction batch processing and payment management, manage supervisory contract hierarchy for product relationship management, handle scheduled instruction and recurring payment automation, access vault-generated financial statement and position data, retrieve audit trail and event streaming via Kafka, manage account opening and KYC/KYB workflow integration, and integrate Vault core banking with payment systems, risk engines, and compliance platforms.
use_cases: Managing account lifecycle from digital banking platform agents
Processing payment and posting instructions from banking automation agents
Configuring financial products via Smart Contracts from product management agents
Integrating core banking events with risk engines from banking operations agents
not_for: Consumer fintech without banking license or major bank partnership
Legacy bank without appetite for full core banking modernization
Simple payment processing without cloud-native core banking infrastructure
best_when: A major bank or fintech using Thought Machine Vault wants AI agents to automate account lifecycle management, payment processing, financial product configuration via Smart Contracts, and risk/compliance integration.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL+REGULATORY RISK: Core banking Smart Contract deployment is mission-critical — automated product configuration changes affect live customer accounts. Automated posting instruction batches must be validated for accounting correctness before submission. Banking automation requires regulatory compliance review for each jurisdiction.

auth.notes: Thought Machine Vault uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. gRPC and REST API available. Kafka event streaming for real-time vault events. Documentation via docs.thoughtmachine.net (requires customer access). Per-bank deployment with dedicated instances. Smart Contracts in Python for financial product logic. No public webhooks — Kafka for event-driven integration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — major bank licensing agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise licensing; significant multi-year contracts for core banking transformation
pricing.notes: London, United Kingdom. Founded 2014. Private ($940M funding, $2.7B valuation). Cloud-native core banking pioneer. 15+ major bank customers including JPMorgan Chase, Standard Chartered, Lloyds, and SEB. Python Smart Contracts for financial product logic. Cloud-native (GCP, AWS). Strong tier-1 bank transformation focus. Competes with Mambu for cloud-native core banking.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per bank deployment; high-throughput transactional design for bank-scale volumes

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL+REGULATORY RISK: Smart Contract changes affect live financial products — require bank change management and regulatory approval for production changes
Smart Contracts in Python — Vault product logic defined in Python Smart Contracts; automation must understand SC lifecycle and testing requirements
gRPC API — Vault has both REST and gRPC APIs; gRPC for high-throughput transactional operations; REST for management operations
Kafka for events — Vault streams all events to Kafka; event-driven integration requires Kafka consumer infrastructure
Tier-1 bank focus — API documentation and onboarding designed for major bank implementations; not self-serve
Dedicated deployments — each bank has dedicated Vault instance; no multi-tenant SaaS; API endpoints are bank-specific
agent_readiness.security_notes: Tier-1 core banking. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. Multi-region. Core banking transaction and account data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Tier-1 core banking. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. Multi-region. Core banking data.
